perturbation.original_text: 1.1 "Affiliate" means any person or entity that controls, is controlled by or is under common control with the specified person or entity, but only so long as such control exists. The term "control" means the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of an entity, whether through ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 "Affiliate" means any person or entity that has a business relationship with, collaborates with, or is strategically aligned with the specified person or entity. The term "control" is not limited to ownership and may include contractual agreements or strategic partnerships that grant significant influence over an entity's management and policies.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by altering the definition of "Affiliate" to encompass entities with business relationships or strategic alignments, rather than just those under direct or indirect control. This conflicts with other sections, such as those dealing with exclusivity or financial obligations, where the original, narrower definition of 'Affiliate' would apply, leading to uncertainty about which entities are bound by those clauses. This contradicts Section 2.4 Sublicenses, where DIALOG may sublicense the foregoing license rights to any of its Affiliates, and DIALOG will be responsible for the observance and performance by all such Affiliates of all of DIALOG's obligations pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.4 Sublicenses. DIALOG may sublicense the foregoing license rights to any of its Affiliates. DIALOG will be responsible for the observance and performance by all such Affiliates of all of DIALOG's obligations pursuant to this Agreement. DIALOG may sublicense the foregoing license rights to Manufacturing Subcontractors solely to the extent necessary and appropriate for them to manufacture, assemble, test and provide support for the Products. DIALOG may not sublicense the foregoing license rights to any other third party without ENERGOUS' prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of "Affiliate" to include entities with business relationships or strategic alignments, which contradicts the original, narrower definition based on direct or indirect control. This broader definition then conflicts with Section 2.4 regarding sublicenses, as DIALOG's responsibility for Affiliates' obligations would extend to a much wider range of entities than initially intended. The original text's definition of affiliate created a legal boundary, while the changed text removes that boundary, creating a direct contradiction with section 2.4 because the defined term used in that section now includes significantly more entities. This alteration introduces uncertainty about which entities DIALOG is responsible for under the agreement, creating a clear conflict and in-text contradiction as described in the explanation, due to the inconsistency introduced by the new definition.

perturbation.original_text: 12.3 Conditions. DIALOG must notify ENERGOUS within thirty (30) business days after receipt of actual notice of any Claim by a third party for which it seeks indemnification; provided, however, any failure or delay in notice will not relieve ENERGOUS of its obligations hereunder except to the extent that ENERGOUS is actually prejudiced by such failure to notify. ENERGOUS will have control and authority with respect to the defense, litigation, compromise or settlement of such third party Claim (except to the extent that any settlement involves any commitments, responsibilities or obligations on the part of DIALOG, in which case such settlement will require the prior written consent of DIALOG, which consent will not be unreasonably delayed, conditioned or withheld). DIALOG will cooperate and provide assistance and information as may reasonably be required by ENERGOUS (but at ENERGOUS' expense) in connection therewith. DIALOG reserves the right to participate at its own cost in any third party proceedings with counsel of its own choosing. In the event that ENERGOUS does not respond to any third party Claim or does not sufficiently defend such third party Claim, DIALOG, acting reasonably, may step in and take over the defense of such Claim. Costs incurred in the settlement of any Claim, including, but not limited to, reasonable legal expenses, may be off set against future Royalties and Service Fees payable.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.3 Conditions. DIALOG must notify ENERGOUS within ninety (90) business days after receipt of actual notice of any Claim by a third party for which it seeks indemnification; provided, however, any failure or delay in notice will relieve ENERGOUS of its obligations hereunder regardless of prejudice. ENERGOUS will have control and authority with respect to the defense, litigation, compromise or settlement of such third party Claim (except to the extent that any settlement involves any commitments, responsibilities or obligations on the part of DIALOG, in which case such settlement will require the prior written consent of DIALOG, which consent will not be unreasonably delayed, conditioned or withheld). DIALOG will cooperate and provide assistance and information as may reasonably be required by ENERGOUS (but at ENERGOUS' expense) in connection therewith. DIALOG reserves the right to participate at its own cost in any third party proceedings with counsel of its own choosing. In the event that ENERGOUS does not respond to any third party Claim or does not sufficiently defend such third party Claim, DIALOG, acting reasonably, may step in and take over the defense of such Claim. Costs incurred in the settlement of any Claim, including, but not limited to, reasonable legal expenses, may be off set against future Royalties and Service Fees payable.
perturbation.explanation: This modification creates an in-text contradiction by changing the notification period for indemnification claims from thirty (30) to ninety (90) business days, and stating that any failure or delay in providing notice will relieve ENERGOUS of its indemnification obligations, regardless of prejudice. This contradicts Section 12.1 Indemnification by Energous, making EnergoUS not liable for DIALOG actions, and is enforced regardless of the severity. This creates ambiguity and potential disputes regarding ENERGOUS' liability for indemnification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.1 Indemnification by Energous. Subject to Section 12.2, ENERGOUS agrees to indemnify, hold harmless and, in the case of any third party claims, defend DIALOG and its Affiliates and each of their respective directors, officers, employees, contractors, agents, distributors and customers (collectively, "DIALOG Indemnitees") from and against and in respect of any and all alleged or actual demands, claims, actions, causes of action, suits or proceedings, assessments, awarded damages (including punitive damages), liabilities, interest and penalties, costs and expenses (including, without limitation, court costs and reasonable legal fees and disbursements in connection therewith) (each, a "Claim") to the extent resulting from, arising out of, relating to, or imposed upon or incurred by any DIALOG Indemnitees by reason of (a) death or bodily injury caused by or resulting from use of the Products, (b) any breach of any representation or warranty made by ENERGOUS hereunder or to any third party in relation to the Products or Product Die, (c) the infringement or misappropriation of any third party Intellectual Property Rights in relation to the Products or Product Die, (d) the infringement or misappropriation of any third party Intellectual Property Rights as a result of DIALOG's or its Affiliates' exercise of rights in accordance with the terms of this Agreement, including, but not limited to, the Manufacturing Subcontractors' manufacture of the Products on their behalf, provided that the Products are manufactured in strict compliance with the Product Specifications and Approved Production Specifications and only to the extent such Claims arise due to compliance with the Product Specifications and/or the Approved Production Specifications or use of the Tooling provided by ENERGOUS hereunder, (e) the infringement by DIALOG of any third party Marks rights as a result of its authorized use of the ENERGOUS Marks, (f) any failure by ENERGOUS to comply with applicable laws, regulations and standards, or (g) ENERGOUS' negligence, intentional misconduct or fraud.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 12.3 to state that "any failure or delay in notice will relieve ENERGOUS of its obligations hereunder regardless of prejudice." This directly contradicts Section 12.1, which outlines ENERGOUS's indemnification obligations. The changed text allows ENERGOUS to avoid these obligations even if the delay doesn't cause prejudice, creating an irreconcilable conflict with Section 12.1, which implicitly assumes ENERGOUS has indemnification obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 9.3 Payment. Payments of Royalties and Service Fees will be due on a calendar quarterly basis, within [***] days after the end of the calendar quarter in which the applicable Licensed Products were Sold or services were rendered. From the date a payment is due, unless otherwise agreed, any late payment will accrue a late payment fee of [***] per month, or the highest interest rate permitted by law, whichever is less.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.3 Payment. Payments of Royalties and Service Fees will be due on a calendar quarterly basis, within [***] days after the end of the calendar quarter in which the applicable Licensed Products were Sold or services were rendered. Late payments will not accrue any late payment fee.
perturbation.explanation: This edit creates an in-text contradiction by stating that late payments will not accrue any late payment fee. This contradicts an earlier statement within the same section stating "From the date a payment is due, unless otherwise agreed, any late payment will accrue a late payment fee of [***] per month, or the highest interest rate permitted by law, whichever is less.", causing uncertainty on the penalty for late payment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.3 Payment. Payments of Royalties and Service Fees will be due on a calendar quarterly basis, within [***] days after the end of the calendar quarter in which the applicable Licensed Products were Sold or services were rendered. Late payments will not accrue any late payment fee.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 9.3 explicitly states that 'Late payments will not accrue any late payment fee', directly contradicting the original text and the contradicted text, which previously stated that late payments would accrue a fee. This creates an in-text contradiction according to the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, as the contract now provides conflicting penalties for late payments, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes.
